title: API Retry Simulator with Exponential Backoff
description:
Simulate an API client with exponential backoff retry logic. Given a list of response codes the API will return (in order), determine the total time elapsed and whether the request ultimately succeeded.

Rules:
- Retry on 429 or 5xx status codes
- Max retries: given as parameter
- Backoff: 2^attempt seconds (attempt starts at 0). So delays are 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s...
- Success: 2xx status code
- Failure: 4xx (except 429) stops immediately
- If retries exhausted, return failure

Return: [success: bool, total_time_seconds: int, attempts: int]

Example:
  Input: responses=[429, 500, 200], max_retries=5
  Output: [true, 3, 3]  (retry after 1s, retry after 2s, success on 3rd attempt = 3s total)
hints: Backoff delay before retry N is 2^(N-1) seconds
author: Claudius
